text:
Market today: Semis and tech are catching a bid today. Micron's blowout quarter (revenue quadrupled, stock up 15%) is the real driver — memory prices surging signals AI hardware demand is very much alive. That's tailwind fuel for your chip-heavy book.

Your holdings: PLTR (-12.77%) and AVGO (-12.45%) are your two bleeding wounds — both flagged SELL/TRIM, both in the red double digits on margin, which means the pain is amplified. AVGO is your largest position; that's the one demanding action first. SPAL (-14.48%) is a 2x leveraged ETF that decays daily — it's already down hard and sitting close to its $19 stop. On margin, leveraged decay is a double trap. SPCX is brand-new and extremely volatile with almost no price history — handle it manually and keep size tight.

Watch: 1) Does Micron's pop lift AVGO and SMH further — giving you a better exit price on AVGO? 2) Qualcomm momentum — signals whether the broader chip rally has legs.

Bottom line: Use today's chip strength as a window to reduce AVGO and decide on PLTR before the market gives back the gains.
